mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling or remains near the zero lower bound, discount rates decline and equity valuation multiples expand, so the S&P 500 should rise over the next month. The signal fails if rate cuts coincide with deteriorating inflation or labor data that overwhelms valuation support.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward returns are negative in a majority of observations when FEDFUNDS is declining, or if the rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03 despite repeated easing episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A CPIAUCSL inflation breakout above recent trend forces tighter policy expectations and a higher real discount rate, which should pressure the S&P 500 lower over the next month. The effect is strongest when inflation is accelerating faster than the labor market can offset it.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20-day SPX returns more than 60% of the time, or if the signal loses sign consistency after controlling for concurrent FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: A rising PAYEMS series indicates broad payroll expansion and stronger household income, which supports earnings expectations and near-term equity returns. This mechanism should predict positive S&P 500 returns over a one-week horizon when payroll gains are persistent rather than one-off.
failure_condition: Reject if consecutive monthly payroll gains are followed by negative 5-day SPX returns in more than 55% of cases, or if the short-horizon Rank IC is indistinguishable from zero over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A jump in UNRATE above its recent path signals cyclical weakening, rising recession risk, and downward revisions to earnings, which should depress the S&P 500 over the next month. The signal is invalid if unemployment rises only because labor supply expands while payrolls remain firm.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than half of unemployment-surge episodes, or if the signal disappears when PAYEMS is stable or rising.

mechanism_narrative: Stronger RSAFS growth reflects resilient consumer spending and revenue momentum, which should lift equity prices over the following month through better sales expectations. The effect should be weakest when retail sales growth is broad but margins are being squeezed by inflation.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month growth turns positive after a negative streak
failure_condition: Reject if positive-turnaround retail sales episodes do not produce above-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if the signal’s rolling Rank IC stays below 0.03 for any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO together with stronger HOUST indicates improving real activity and construction-linked supply conditions, supporting cyclical earnings and risk appetite. The mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter when both indicators accelerate simultaneously.
failure_condition: Reject if joint industrial-production and housing-starts upswings are followed by negative 60-day SPX returns more than 50% of the time, or if either proxy alone explains the result better than the interaction.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in HOUST after a prior expansion signals housing demand rollover and a broader slowdown in interest-sensitive consumption, which should lower the S&P 500 over the next month. The signal fails if housing weakness is offset by strong retail sales or payroll growth.
failure_condition: Reject if housing-start declines trigger positive 20-day SPX returns in a majority of cases, or if the combined HOUST-RSAFS pattern has no incremental predictive power versus RSAFS alone.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE is low and PAYEMS is still rising, the labor market remains tight without immediate recession stress, supporting earnings and investor confidence over the next quarter. This regime should produce positive S&P 500 returns unless inflation and policy tightening dominate.
failure_condition: Reject if low-unemployment plus payroll-growth regimes do not outperform baseline over 60-day horizons, or if the sign flips negative whenever FEDFUNDS is stable rather than tightening.
